pattern: Since moves only decrease values, each candidate parity of valleys is independent, so the cost of pushing each chosen-valley below both neighbors is summed and the cheaper parity wins.
hints: You only ever decrease numbers, so a position you want to be a valley must drop below both of its neighbors; the neighbors stay put.
There are exactly two target shapes: valleys on even indices or valleys on odd indices. Compute the cost of each separately.
For each parity, sum over the chosen valley positions max(0, value - min(left, right) + 1), then return the smaller of the two totals.

edge_inputs.description: all equal forces a strict inequality cost

stress.note: values bounded so the two-parity cost stays well-defined
